title: 2026 Oscars Best Original Screenplay Predictions
text: Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" is predicted to win the Best Original Screenplay Oscar at the 2026 Academy Awards. The Frontrunner: Coogler appears positioned for victory, which would make him only the second Black writer to win in this category after Jordan Peele's win for "Get Out" in 2017. Historic Achievement: The article notes that "Sinners" marks the sixth film with a Black writer nominated for original screenplay. Should Coogler prevail, he would join an exclusive group, as he's already the third Black person nominated simultaneously for producing, directing, and screenwriting in the same year—following Peele and Spike Lee. Supporting Context: The prediction commentary emphasizes that both Coogler and adapted screenplay competitor Paul Thomas Anderson ("One Battle After Another") are "sure-fire" candidates to win Oscars in their respective categories. The 98th Academy Awards ceremony is scheduled for March 15, 2026, hosted by Conan O'Brien. Variety's Awards Circuit section updates predictions weekly based on evolving industry sentiment and formal polling data.
